offer_name: Self-hosted ZMath Shield / Q Call secure-comms license

standard_matrix_chat.description: Standard Matrix-compatible messaging stays free on CallChat.

hosted_callchat_shield.user_experience: After one local unlock with a passphrase and exact pattern image, the hosted Element client automatically protects composer messages and selected attachments before send and opens matching incoming messages while unlocked.
hosted_callchat_shield.factor_handling: The image may be remembered only as an AES-GCM encrypted local device record. The passphrase remains session-only and is never stored.
hosted_callchat_shield.compatibility: Legacy ZSHIELD-PBKDF2-AESGCM-1 containers remain readable. Matrix-only sending is an explicit user choice.
hosted_callchat_shield.recipient_requirement: Recipients need the same passphrase and exact pattern image for the additional ZMath layer.
hosted_callchat_shield.public_boundary: The working MVP uses reviewable standard Web Crypto primitives. Premium policy, managed recovery, and private ZMath research remain separate.

self_hosted_zmath_shield_license.scope: One approved self-hosted CallChat-compatible deployment on one public server IP with unlimited users
includes: owner-controlled MatrixRTC and Q Call posture

security_boundary: No plaintext files in this public status endpoint.
No Shield passwords, pattern material, derived keys, policy bearer secrets, or private ZMath source are published here.
The hosted client performs ZMath envelope encryption locally in the browser; private research and server credentials remain outside the public tree.
